authors_text: Mehrdad Fazli,Kamran Kowsari,Erfaneh Gharavi,Laura Barnes,Afsaneh Doryab
title: HHAR-net: Hierarchical Human Activity Recognition using Neural Networks
title_zh: HHAR-net：使用神经网络进行分层人类活动识别
abstract: Activity recognition using built-in sensors in smart and wearable devices provides great opportunities to understand and detect human behavior in the wild and gives a more holistic view of individuals' health and well being. Numerous computational methods have been applied to sensor streams to recognize different daily activities. However, most methods are unable to capture different layers of activities concealed in human behavior. Also, the performance of the models starts to decrease with increasing the number of activities. This research aims at building a hierarchical classification with Neural Networks to recognize human activities based on different levels of abstraction. We evaluate our model on the Extrasensory dataset; a dataset collected in the wild and containing data from smartphones and smartwatches. We use a two-level hierarchy with a total of six mutually exclusive labels namely, "lying down", "sitting", "standing in place", "walking", "running", and "bicycling" divided into "stationary" and "non-stationary". The results show that our model can recognize low-level activities (stationary/non-stationary) with 95.8% accuracy and overall accuracy of 92.8% over six labels. This is 3% above our best performing baseline.
summary: Activity recognition using built-in sensors in smart and wearable devices provides great opportunities to understand and detect human behavior in the wild and gives a more holistic view of individuals' health and well being. Numerous computational methods have been applied to sensor streams to recognize different daily activities. However, most methods are unable to capture different layers of activities concealed in human behavior. Also, the performance of the models starts to decrease with increasing the number of activities. This research aims at building a hierarchical classification with Neural Networks to recognize human activities based on different levels of abstraction. We evaluate our model on the Extrasensory dataset; a dataset collected in the wild and containing data from smartphones and smartwatches. We use a two-level hierarchy with a total of six mutually exclusive labels namely, "lying down", "sitting", "standing in place", "walking", "running", and "bicycling" divided into "stationary" and "non-stationary". The results show that our model can recognize low-level activities (stationary/non-stationary) with 95.8% accuracy and overall accuracy of 92.8% over six labels. This is 3% above our best performing baseline.
abstract_zh: 使用智能和可穿戴设备中的内置传感器进行活动识别为理解和检测人类在野外的行为提供了绝佳的机会，并更全面地了解个人的健康和福祉。许多计算方法已应用于传感器流以识别不同的日常活动。然而，大多数方法无法捕获人类行为中隐藏的不同层次的活动。此外，随着活动数量的增加，模型的性能开始下降。本研究旨在利用神经网络构建层次分类，以根据不同的抽象级别识别人类活动。我们在 Extrasensory 数据集上评估我们的模型；在野外收集的数据集，包含来自智能手机和智能手表的数据。我们使用两级层次结构，共有六个互斥的标签，即“躺着”、“坐着”、“站立”、“行走”、“跑步”和“骑自行车”，分为“静止”和“非静止”。结果表明，我们的模型可以识别低级别活动（静态/非静态），准确率达 95.8%，六个标签的总体准确率达 92.8%。这比我们的最佳表现基准高出 3%。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于神经网络的层次化人类活动识别模型HHAR-net，用于通过智能设备传感器数据识别不同层次的人类活动。
AI.motivation: 现有方法难以捕捉人类行为中的多层次活动信息，且活动类别增加时模型性能下降，需通过层次化分类提升识别效果。
AI.method: 构建两层层次化分类结构，将六类活动（躺下、坐着、站立、走路、跑步、骑车）划分为静态/非静态两类，基于Extrasensory数据集训练神经网络模型。
AI.result: 模型在低层次活动识别准确率达95.8%，六类活动总体准确率为92.8%，较最佳基线模型提升3%。
AI.conclusion: 层次化神经网络有效提升了多类别活动识别性能，验证了分层抽象对复杂行为模式识别的优越性。

authors_text: Alberto Barbado,Óscar Corcho
title: Interpretable Machine Learning Models for Predicting and Explaining Vehicle Fuel Consumption Anomalies
title_zh: 用于预测和解释车辆燃油消耗异常的可解释机器学习模型
abstract: Identifying anomalies in the fuel consumption of the vehicles of a fleet is a crucial aspect for optimizing consumption and reduce costs. However, this information alone is insufficient, since fleet operators need to know the causes behind anomalous fuel consumption. We combine unsupervised anomaly detection techniques, domain knowledge and interpretable Machine Learning models for explaining potential causes of abnormal fuel consumption in terms of feature relevance. The explanations are used for generating recommendations about fuel optimization, that are adjusted according to two different user profiles: fleet managers and fleet operators. Results are evaluated over real-world data from telematics devices connected to diesel and petrol vehicles from different types of industrial fleets. We measure the proposal regarding model performance, and using Explainable AI metrics that compare the explanations in terms of representativeness, fidelity, stability, contrastiveness and consistency with apriori beliefs. The potential fuel reductions that can be achieved is round 35%.
summary: Identifying anomalies in the fuel consumption of the vehicles of a fleet is a crucial aspect for optimizing consumption and reduce costs. However, this information alone is insufficient, since fleet operators need to know the causes behind anomalous fuel consumption. We combine unsupervised anomaly detection techniques, domain knowledge and interpretable Machine Learning models for explaining potential causes of abnormal fuel consumption in terms of feature relevance. The explanations are used for generating recommendations about fuel optimization, that are adjusted according to two different user profiles: fleet managers and fleet operators. Results are evaluated over real-world data from telematics devices connected to diesel and petrol vehicles from different types of industrial fleets. We measure the proposal regarding model performance, and using Explainable AI metrics that compare the explanations in terms of representativeness, fidelity, stability, contrastiveness and consistency with apriori beliefs. The potential fuel reductions that can be achieved is round 35%.
abstract_zh: 识别车队车辆燃油消耗的异常情况是优化消耗和降低成本的一个重要方面。然而，仅此信息是不够的，因为车队运营商需要了解异常燃油消耗背后的原因。我们结合无监督异常检测技术、领域知识和可解释的机器学习模型，从特征相关性方面解释异常燃油消耗的潜在原因。这些解释用于生成有关燃料优化的建议，这些建议根据两个不同的用户配置文件进行调整：车队经理和车队运营商。结果是根据连接到不同类型工业车队的柴油和汽油车辆的远程信息处理设备的真实数据进行评估的。我们衡量有关模型性能的建议，并使用可解释的人工智能指标来比较代表性、保真度、稳定性、对比性和与先验信念的一致性方面的解释。可以实现的潜在燃料减少量约为 35%。

AI.tldr: 本文提出结合可解释机器学习模型，预测并解释车队车辆燃油消耗异常，以优化燃油使用并降低成本。
AI.motivation: 检测燃油消耗异常虽重要，但车队运营商更需了解异常背后的原因以采取改进措施，现有方法缺乏对原因的解释能力。
AI.method: 融合无监督异常检测技术、领域知识和可解释机器学习模型，通过特征相关性分析解释异常原因，并针对车队管理者和操作员两类用户生成优化建议。
AI.result: 基于真实车载终端数据的实验显示，该方法可实现约35%的潜在燃油节约，且解释结果在代表性、保真度、稳定性等指标上表现良好。
AI.conclusion: 该方法有效识别并解释燃油异常原因，为不同用户角色提供可操作的优化建议，显著提升燃油效率。

authors_text: Shahin Heidarian,Parnian Afshar,Arash Mohammadi,Moezedin Javad Rafiee,Anastasia Oikonomou,Konstantinos N. Plataniotis,Farnoosh Naderkhani
title: CT-CAPS: Feature Extraction-based Automated Framework for COVID-19 Disease Identification from Chest CT Scans using Capsule Networks
title_zh: CT-CAPS：使用胶囊网络从胸部 CT 扫描中识别 COVID-19 疾病的基于特征提取的自动化框架
abstract: The global outbreak of the novel corona virus (COVID-19) disease has drastically impacted the world and led to one of the most challenging crisis across the globe since World War II. The early diagnosis and isolation of COVID-19 positive cases are considered as crucial steps towards preventing the spread of the disease and flattening the epidemic curve. Chest Computed Tomography (CT) scan is a highly sensitive, rapid, and accurate diagnostic technique that can complement Reverse Transcription Polymerase Chain Reaction (RT-PCR) test. Recently, deep learning-based models, mostly based on Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), have shown promising diagnostic results. CNNs, however, are incapable of capturing spatial relations between image instances and require large datasets. Capsule Networks, on the other hand, can capture spatial relations, require smaller datasets, and have considerably fewer parameters. In this paper, a Capsule network framework, referred to as the "CT-CAPS", is presented to automatically extract distinctive features of chest CT scans. These features, which are extracted from the layer before the final capsule layer, are then leveraged to differentiate COVID-19 from Non-COVID cases. The experiments on our in-house dataset of 307 patients show the state-of-the-art performance with the accuracy of 90.8%, sensitivity of 94.5%, and specificity of 86.0%.
summary: The global outbreak of the novel corona virus (COVID-19) disease has drastically impacted the world and led to one of the most challenging crisis across the globe since World War II. The early diagnosis and isolation of COVID-19 positive cases are considered as crucial steps towards preventing the spread of the disease and flattening the epidemic curve. Chest Computed Tomography (CT) scan is a highly sensitive, rapid, and accurate diagnostic technique that can complement Reverse Transcription Polymerase Chain Reaction (RT-PCR) test. Recently, deep learning-based models, mostly based on Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), have shown promising diagnostic results. CNNs, however, are incapable of capturing spatial relations between image instances and require large datasets. Capsule Networks, on the other hand, can capture spatial relations, require smaller datasets, and have considerably fewer parameters. In this paper, a Capsule network framework, referred to as the "CT-CAPS", is presented to automatically extract distinctive features of chest CT scans. These features, which are extracted from the layer before the final capsule layer, are then leveraged to differentiate COVID-19 from Non-COVID cases. The experiments on our in-house dataset of 307 patients show the state-of-the-art performance with the accuracy of 90.8%, sensitivity of 94.5%, and specificity of 86.0%.
abstract_zh: 新型冠状病毒（COVID-19）疾病的全球爆发对世界产生了巨大影响，并导致了二战以来全球最具挑战性的危机之一。 COVID-19阳性病例的早期诊断和隔离被认为是防止疾病传播和拉平流行曲线的关键步骤。胸部计算机断层扫描 (CT) 扫描是一种高度灵敏、快速且准确的诊断技术，可以补充逆转录聚合酶链反应 (RT-PCR) 测试。最近，基于深度学习的模型（主要基于卷积神经网络（CNN））已经显示出有希望的诊断结果。然而，CNN 无法捕获图像实例之间的空间关系，并且需要大量数据集。另一方面，胶囊网络可以捕获空间关系，需要较小的数据集，并且参数要少得多。在本文中，提出了一种称为“CT-CAPS”的胶囊网络框架，用于自动提取胸部 CT 扫描的独特特征。这些特征是从最终胶囊层之前的层中提取的，然后利用这些特征来区分 COVID-19 和非 COVID 病例。我们对 307 名患者的内部数据集进行的实验显示了最先进的性能，准确度为 90.8%，敏感性为 94.5%，特异性为 86.0%。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于胶囊网络的自动框架CT-CAPS，用于从胸部CT扫描中识别COVID-19疾病。
AI.motivation: 传统CNN在捕捉图像空间关系和数据需求方面存在局限，而胶囊网络能更高效地提取特征且需要更小数据集，因此提出CT-CAPS以提升诊断效率和准确性。
AI.method: 设计CT-CAPS框架，利用胶囊网络自动提取胸部CT特征，通过最终胶囊层前的特征区分COVID-19与非COVID病例。
AI.result: 在307例患者数据集上达到90.8%的准确率、94.5%的灵敏度和86.0%的特异性，表现优于现有方法。
AI.conclusion: CT-CAPS框架通过胶囊网络有效提取特征，在较小数据集下实现高精度的COVID-19诊断，具有临床应用潜力。

search_text: CT-CAPS: Feature Extraction-based Automated Framework for COVID-19 Disease Identification from Chest CT Scans using Capsule Networks CT-CAPS：使用胶囊网络从胸部 CT 扫描中识别 COVID-19 疾病的基于特征提取的自动化框架 The global outbreak of the novel corona virus (COVID-19) disease has drastically impacted the world and led to one of the most challenging crisis across the globe since World War II. The early diagnosis and isolation of COVID-19 positive cases are considered as crucial steps towards preventing the spread of the disease and flattening the epidemic curve. Chest Computed Tomography (CT) scan is a highly sensitive, rapid, and accurate diagnostic technique that can complement Reverse Transcription Polymerase Chain Reaction (RT-PCR) test. Recently, deep learning-based models, mostly based on Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), have shown promising diagnostic results. CNNs, however, are incapable of capturing spatial relations between image instances and require large datasets. Capsule Networks, on the other hand, can capture spatial relations, require smaller datasets, and have considerably fewer parameters. In this paper, a Capsule network framework, referred to as the "CT-CAPS", is presented to automatically extract distinctive features of chest CT scans. These features, which are extracted from the layer before the final capsule layer, are then leveraged to differentiate COVID-19 from Non-COVID cases. The experiments on our in-house dataset of 307 patients show the state-of-the-art performance with the accuracy of 90.8%, sensitivity of 94.5%, and specificity of 86.0%. 新型冠状病毒（COVID-19）疾病的全球爆发对世界产生了巨大影响，并导致了二战以来全球最具挑战性的危机之一。 COVID-19阳性病例的早期诊断和隔离被认为是防止疾病传播和拉平流行曲线的关键步骤。胸部计算机断层扫描 (CT) 扫描是一种高度灵敏、快速且准确的诊断技术，可以补充逆转录聚合酶链反应 (RT-PCR) 测试。最近，基于深度学习的模型（主要基于卷积神经网络（CNN））已经显示出有希望的诊断结果。然而，CNN 无法捕获图像实例之间的空间关系，并且需要大量数据集。另一方面，胶囊网络可以捕获空间关系，需要较小的数据集，并且参数要少得多。在本文中，提出了一种称为“CT-CAPS”的胶囊网络框架，用于自动提取胸部 CT 扫描的独特特征。这些特征是从最终胶囊层之前的层中提取的，然后利用这些特征来区分 COVID-19 和非 COVID 病例。我们对 307 名患者的内部数据集进行的实验显示了最先进的性能，准确度为 90.8%，敏感性为 94.5%，特异性为 86.0%。 本文提出了一种基于胶囊网络的自动框架CT-CAPS，用于从胸部CT扫描中识别COVID-19疾病。 Shahin Heidarian,Parnian Afshar,Arash Mohammadi,Moezedin Javad Rafiee,Anastasia Oikonomou,Konstantinos N. Plataniotis,Farnoosh Naderkhani eess.IV,cs.CV,cs.LG

authors_text: Shahin Heidarian,Parnian Afshar,Nastaran Enshaei,Farnoosh Naderkhani,Anastasia Oikonomou,S. Farokh Atashzar,Faranak Babaki Fard,Kaveh Samimi,Konstantinos N. Plataniotis,Arash Mohammadi,Moezedin Javad Rafiee
title: COVID-FACT: A Fully-Automated Capsule Network-based Framework for Identification of COVID-19 Cases from Chest CT scans
title_zh: COVID-FACT：基于全自动胶囊网络的框架，用于通过胸部 CT 扫描识别 COVID-19 病例
abstract: The newly discovered Corona virus Disease 2019 (COVID-19) has been globally spreading and causing hundreds of thousands of deaths around the world as of its first emergence in late 2019. Computed tomography (CT) scans have shown distinctive features and higher sensitivity compared to other diagnostic tests, in particular the current gold standard, i.e., the Reverse Transcription Polymerase Chain Reaction (RT-PCR) test. Current deep learning-based algorithms are mainly developed based on Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) to identify COVID-19 pneumonia cases. CNNs, however, require extensive data augmentation and large datasets to identify detailed spatial relations between image instances. Furthermore, existing algorithms utilizing CT scans, either extend slice-level predictions to patient-level ones using a simple thresholding mechanism or rely on a sophisticated infection segmentation to identify the disease. In this paper, we propose a two-stage fully-automated CT-based framework for identification of COVID-19 positive cases referred to as the "COVID-FACT". COVID-FACT utilizes Capsule Networks, as its main building blocks and is, therefore, capable of capturing spatial information. In particular, to make the proposed COVID-FACT independent from sophisticated segmentation of the area of infection, slices demonstrating infection are detected at the first stage and the second stage is responsible for classifying patients into COVID and non-COVID cases. COVID-FACT detects slices with infection, and identifies positive COVID-19 cases using an in-house CT scan dataset, containing COVID-19, community acquired pneumonia, and normal cases. Based on our experiments, COVID-FACT achieves an accuracy of 90.82%, a sensitivity of 94.55%, a specificity of 86.04%, and an Area Under the Curve (AUC) of 0.98, while depending on far less supervision and annotation, in comparison to its counterparts.
summary: The newly discovered Corona virus Disease 2019 (COVID-19) has been globally spreading and causing hundreds of thousands of deaths around the world as of its first emergence in late 2019. Computed tomography (CT) scans have shown distinctive features and higher sensitivity compared to other diagnostic tests, in particular the current gold standard, i.e., the Reverse Transcription Polymerase Chain Reaction (RT-PCR) test. Current deep learning-based algorithms are mainly developed based on Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) to identify COVID-19 pneumonia cases. CNNs, however, require extensive data augmentation and large datasets to identify detailed spatial relations between image instances. Furthermore, existing algorithms utilizing CT scans, either extend slice-level predictions to patient-level ones using a simple thresholding mechanism or rely on a sophisticated infection segmentation to identify the disease. In this paper, we propose a two-stage fully-automated CT-based framework for identification of COVID-19 positive cases referred to as the "COVID-FACT". COVID-FACT utilizes Capsule Networks, as its main building blocks and is, therefore, capable of capturing spatial information. In particular, to make the proposed COVID-FACT independent from sophisticated segmentation of the area of infection, slices demonstrating infection are detected at the first stage and the second stage is responsible for classifying patients into COVID and non-COVID cases. COVID-FACT detects slices with infection, and identifies positive COVID-19 cases using an in-house CT scan dataset, containing COVID-19, community acquired pneumonia, and normal cases. Based on our experiments, COVID-FACT achieves an accuracy of 90.82%, a sensitivity of 94.55%, a specificity of 86.04%, and an Area Under the Curve (AUC) of 0.98, while depending on far less supervision and annotation, in comparison to its counterparts.
abstract_zh: 新发现的 2019 冠状病毒病 (COVID-19) 自 2019 年底首次出现以来，已在全球范围内传播，并导致数十万人死亡。与其他诊断测试，特别是当前的金标准，即逆转录聚合酶链反应 (RT-PCR) 测试相比，计算机断层扫描 (CT) 扫描显示出独特的特征和更高的灵敏度。目前基于深度学习的算法主要基于卷积神经网络（CNN）开发来识别 COVID-19 肺炎病例。然而，CNN 需要大量的数据增强和大型数据集来识别图像实例之间的详细空间关系。此外，利用 CT 扫描的现有算法要么使用简单的阈值机制将切片级预测扩展到患者级预测，要么依赖复杂的感染分割来识别疾病。在本文中，我们提出了一种基于 CT 的两阶段全自动框架，用于识别 COVID-19 阳性病例，称为“COVID-FACT”。 COVID-FACT 利用胶囊网络作为其主要构建块，因此能够捕获空间信息。特别是，为了使拟议的 COVID-FACT 独立于感染区域的复杂分割，在第一阶段检测显示感染的切片，第二阶段负责将患者分为 COVID 和非 COVID 病例。 COVID-FACT 可检测感染的切片，并使用内部 CT 扫描数据集（包含 COVID-19、社区获得性肺炎和正常病例）识别阳性 COVID-19 病例。根据我们的实验，与同类产品相比，COVID-FACT 的准确度为 90.82%，敏感性为 94.55%，特异性为 86.04%，曲线下面积 (AUC) 为 0.98，同时依赖的监督和注释要少得多。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于胶囊网络的全自动框架（COVID-FACT），用于通过胸部CT扫描识别COVID-19病例。
AI.motivation: 现有基于卷积神经网络（CNN）的算法需要大量数据增强和标注，且依赖复杂的分割或简单阈值机制，效率较低。本文旨在开发一种更高效、依赖更少标注的解决方案。
AI.method: 采用两阶段胶囊网络框架：第一阶段检测感染切片，第二阶段分类患者为COVID-19或非COVID-19病例，无需复杂分割即可捕捉空间信息。
AI.result: 在自建数据集上达到90.82%的准确率、94.55%的灵敏度、86.04%的特异度和0.98的AUC，且依赖更少的监督和标注。
AI.conclusion: COVID-FACT在较少标注情况下实现了高精度诊断，证明了胶囊网络在医学影像分析中的有效性。

authors_text: Shufeng Kong,Junwen Bai,Jae Hee Lee,Di Chen,Andrew Allyn,Michelle Stuart,Malin Pinsky,Katherine Mills,Carla P. Gomes
title: Deep Hurdle Networks for Zero-Inflated Multi-Target Regression: Application to Multiple Species Abundance Estimation
title_zh: 用于零膨胀多目标回归的深度障碍网络：在多物种丰度估计中的应用
abstract: A key problem in computational sustainability is to understand the distribution of species across landscapes over time. This question gives rise to challenging large-scale prediction problems since (i) hundreds of species have to be simultaneously modeled and (ii) the survey data are usually inflated with zeros due to the absence of species for a large number of sites. The problem of tackling both issues simultaneously, which we refer to as the zero-inflated multi-target regression problem, has not been addressed by previous methods in statistics and machine learning. In this paper, we propose a novel deep model for the zero-inflated multi-target regression problem. To this end, we first model the joint distribution of multiple response variables as a multivariate probit model and then couple the positive outcomes with a multivariate log-normal distribution. By penalizing the difference between the two distributions' covariance matrices, a link between both distributions is established. The whole model is cast as an end-to-end learning framework and we provide an efficient learning algorithm for our model that can be fully implemented on GPUs. We show that our model outperforms the existing state-of-the-art baselines on two challenging real-world species distribution datasets concerning bird and fish populations.
summary: A key problem in computational sustainability is to understand the distribution of species across landscapes over time. This question gives rise to challenging large-scale prediction problems since (i) hundreds of species have to be simultaneously modeled and (ii) the survey data are usually inflated with zeros due to the absence of species for a large number of sites. The problem of tackling both issues simultaneously, which we refer to as the zero-inflated multi-target regression problem, has not been addressed by previous methods in statistics and machine learning. In this paper, we propose a novel deep model for the zero-inflated multi-target regression problem. To this end, we first model the joint distribution of multiple response variables as a multivariate probit model and then couple the positive outcomes with a multivariate log-normal distribution. By penalizing the difference between the two distributions' covariance matrices, a link between both distributions is established. The whole model is cast as an end-to-end learning framework and we provide an efficient learning algorithm for our model that can be fully implemented on GPUs. We show that our model outperforms the existing state-of-the-art baselines on two challenging real-world species distribution datasets concerning bird and fish populations.
abstract_zh: 计算可持续性的一个关键问题是了解物种随时间在景观中的分布。这个问题引起了具有挑战性的大规模预测问题，因为（i）必须同时对数百个物种进行建模，并且（ii）由于大量地点缺乏物种，调查数据通常会被夸大为零。同时解决这两个问题的问题，我们称之为零膨胀多目标回归问题，以前的统计和机器学习方法尚未解决。在本文中，我们针对零膨胀多目标回归问题提出了一种新颖的深度模型。为此，我们首先将多个响应变量的联合分布建模为多元概率模型，然后将正结果与多元对数正态分布耦合。通过惩罚两个分布的协方差矩阵之间的差异，建立两个分布之间的联系。整个模型被构建为一个端到端的学习框架，我们为我们的模型提供了一个可以在 GPU 上完全实现的高效学习算法。我们表明，我们的模型在两个有关鸟类和鱼类种群的具有挑战性的现实世界物种分布数据集上优于现有的最先进基线。

AI.tldr: 本文提出深度Hurdle网络解决零膨胀多目标回归问题，用于多个物种丰度估计。
AI.motivation: 传统方法无法同时处理多物种建模与零值数据问题，导致预测准确性不足。
AI.method: 结合多元probit模型与多元对数正态分布，通过协方差矩阵惩罚建立联系，构建端到端学习框架。
AI.result: 在鸟类和鱼类种群数据集上优于现有先进方法，验证模型有效性。
AI.conclusion: 该方法有效解决零膨胀多目标回归挑战，为物种分布预测提供新思路。

search_text: Deep Hurdle Networks for Zero-Inflated Multi-Target Regression: Application to Multiple Species Abundance Estimation 用于零膨胀多目标回归的深度障碍网络：在多物种丰度估计中的应用 A key problem in computational sustainability is to understand the distribution of species across landscapes over time. This question gives rise to challenging large-scale prediction problems since (i) hundreds of species have to be simultaneously modeled and (ii) the survey data are usually inflated with zeros due to the absence of species for a large number of sites. The problem of tackling both issues simultaneously, which we refer to as the zero-inflated multi-target regression problem, has not been addressed by previous methods in statistics and machine learning. In this paper, we propose a novel deep model for the zero-inflated multi-target regression problem. To this end, we first model the joint distribution of multiple response variables as a multivariate probit model and then couple the positive outcomes with a multivariate log-normal distribution. By penalizing the difference between the two distributions' covariance matrices, a link between both distributions is established. The whole model is cast as an end-to-end learning framework and we provide an efficient learning algorithm for our model that can be fully implemented on GPUs. We show that our model outperforms the existing state-of-the-art baselines on two challenging real-world species distribution datasets concerning bird and fish populations. 计算可持续性的一个关键问题是了解物种随时间在景观中的分布。这个问题引起了具有挑战性的大规模预测问题，因为（i）必须同时对数百个物种进行建模，并且（ii）由于大量地点缺乏物种，调查数据通常会被夸大为零。同时解决这两个问题的问题，我们称之为零膨胀多目标回归问题，以前的统计和机器学习方法尚未解决。在本文中，我们针对零膨胀多目标回归问题提出了一种新颖的深度模型。为此，我们首先将多个响应变量的联合分布建模为多元概率模型，然后将正结果与多元对数正态分布耦合。通过惩罚两个分布的协方差矩阵之间的差异，建立两个分布之间的联系。整个模型被构建为一个端到端的学习框架，我们为我们的模型提供了一个可以在 GPU 上完全实现的高效学习算法。我们表明，我们的模型在两个有关鸟类和鱼类种群的具有挑战性的现实世界物种分布数据集上优于现有的最先进基线。 本文提出深度Hurdle网络解决零膨胀多目标回归问题，用于多个物种丰度估计。 Shufeng Kong,Junwen Bai,Jae Hee Lee,Di Chen,Andrew Allyn,Michelle Stuart,Malin Pinsky,Katherine Mills,Carla P. authors_text: Zhi Qiao,Austin Bae,Lucas M. Glass,Cao Xiao,Jimeng Sun
title: FLANNEL: Focal Loss Based Neural Network Ensemble for COVID-19 Detection
title_zh: FLANNEL：用于 COVID-19 检测的基于焦点损失的神经网络集成
abstract: To test the possibility of differentiating chest x-ray images of COVID-19 against other pneumonia and healthy patients using deep neural networks. We construct the X-ray imaging data from two publicly available sources, which include 5508 chest x-ray images across 2874 patients with four classes: normal, bacterial pneumonia, non-COVID-19 viral pneumonia, and COVID-19. To identify COVID-19, we propose a Focal Loss Based Neural Ensemble Network (FLANNEL), a flexible module to ensemble several convolutional neural network (CNN) models and fuse with a focal loss for accurate COVID-19 detection on class imbalance data. FLANNEL consistently outperforms baseline models on COVID-19 identification task in all metrics. Compared with the best baseline, FLANNEL shows a higher macro-F1 score with 6% relative increase on Covid-19 identification task where it achieves 0.7833(0.07) in Precision, 0.8609(0.03) in Recall, and 0.8168(0.03) F1 score.
summary: To test the possibility of differentiating chest x-ray images of COVID-19 against other pneumonia and healthy patients using deep neural networks. We construct the X-ray imaging data from two publicly available sources, which include 5508 chest x-ray images across 2874 patients with four classes: normal, bacterial pneumonia, non-COVID-19 viral pneumonia, and COVID-19. To identify COVID-19, we propose a Focal Loss Based Neural Ensemble Network (FLANNEL), a flexible module to ensemble several convolutional neural network (CNN) models and fuse with a focal loss for accurate COVID-19 detection on class imbalance data. FLANNEL consistently outperforms baseline models on COVID-19 identification task in all metrics. Compared with the best baseline, FLANNEL shows a higher macro-F1 score with 6% relative increase on Covid-19 identification task where it achieves 0.7833(0.07) in Precision, 0.8609(0.03) in Recall, and 0.8168(0.03) F1 score.
abstract_zh: 测试使用深度神经网络区分 COVID-19 胸部 X 射线图像与其他肺炎和健康患者的可能性。我们从两个公开来源构建了 X 射线成像数据，其中包括 2874 名患者的 5508 张胸部 X 射线图像，分为四类：正常、细菌性肺炎、非 COVID-19 病毒性肺炎和 COVID-19。为了识别 COVID-19，我们提出了一种基于焦点损失的神经集成网络 (FLANNEL)，这是一种灵活的模块，可集成多个卷积神经网络 (CNN) 模型，并与焦点损失融合，以便对类别不平衡数据进行准确的 COVID-19 检测。 FLANNEL 在所有指标中始终优于 COVID-19 识别任务的基线模型。与最佳基线相比，FLANNEL 在 Covid-19 识别任务中表现出更高的宏观 F1 分数，相对提高了 6%，其精确度达到 0.7833（0.07），召回率达到 0.8609（0.03），F1 分数达到 0.8168（0.03）。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于焦点损失的神经网络集成方法FLANNEL，用于通过胸部X光图像检测COVID-19。
AI.motivation: 由于胸部X光图像中COVID-19与其它肺炎及健康样本的类别不平衡问题，传统的深度学习方法难以准确区分，因此需要一种更有效的模型。
AI.method: 作者构建了一个包含5508张胸部X光图像的数据集，并提出FLANNEL方法，该方法通过集成多个卷积神经网络模型并结合焦点损失函数，以提高在类别不平衡数据上的检测性能。
AI.result: FLANNEL在所有评估指标上均优于基线模型，在COVID-19检测任务中，其F1分数比最佳基线模型提高了6%，达到0.8168。
AI.conclusion: FLANNEL方法在处理类别不平衡数据时表现优异，能够有效提高COVID-19的检测准确率。

authors_text: Wei Li,Yuanjun Xiong,Shuo Yang,Siqi Deng,Wei Xia
title: SMOT: Single-Shot Multi Object Tracking
title_zh: SMOT：单次多目标跟踪
abstract: We present single-shot multi-object tracker (SMOT), a new tracking framework that converts any single-shot detector (SSD) model into an online multiple object tracker, which emphasizes simultaneously detecting and tracking of the object paths. Contrary to the existing tracking by detection approaches which suffer from errors made by the object detectors, SMOT adopts the recently proposed scheme of tracking by re-detection. We combine this scheme with SSD detectors by proposing a novel tracking anchor assignment module. With this design SMOT is able to generate tracklets with a constant per-frame runtime. A light-weighted linkage algorithm is then used for online tracklet linking. On three benchmarks of object tracking: Hannah, Music Videos, and MOT17, the proposed SMOT achieves state-of-the-art performance.
summary: We present single-shot multi-object tracker (SMOT), a new tracking framework that converts any single-shot detector (SSD) model into an online multiple object tracker, which emphasizes simultaneously detecting and tracking of the object paths. Contrary to the existing tracking by detection approaches which suffer from errors made by the object detectors, SMOT adopts the recently proposed scheme of tracking by re-detection. We combine this scheme with SSD detectors by proposing a novel tracking anchor assignment module. With this design SMOT is able to generate tracklets with a constant per-frame runtime. A light-weighted linkage algorithm is then used for online tracklet linking. On three benchmarks of object tracking: Hannah, Music Videos, and MOT17, the proposed SMOT achieves state-of-the-art performance.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了单次多目标跟踪器（SMOT），这是一种新的跟踪框架，可将任何单次检测器（SSD）模型转换为在线多目标跟踪器，强调同时检测和跟踪目标路径。与现有的检测跟踪方法会受到目标检测器误差的影响，SMOT 采用了最近提出的重新检测跟踪方案。我们通过提出一种新颖的跟踪锚分配模块将该方案与 SSD 检测器结合起来。通过这种设计，SMOT 能够生成具有恒定每帧运行时间的轨迹。然后使用轻量级链接算法进行在线轨迹链接。在三个目标跟踪基准：Hannah、音乐视频和 MOT17 上，所提出的 SMOT 实现了最先进的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出SMOT框架，将单阶段检测器转化为在线多目标跟踪器，实现检测与跟踪的同步。
AI.motivation: 现有基于检测的跟踪方法易受检测器误差影响，而SMOT采用重新检测方案以提升跟踪鲁棒性。
AI.method: 设计跟踪锚点分配模块结合单阶段检测器，并采用轻量级算法实现在线轨迹链接。
AI.result: 在Hannah、Music Videos和MOT17三个基准测试中达到最先进水平。
AI.conclusion: SMOT通过创新设计实现了高效准确的多目标跟踪，具有实际应用价值。

We combine this scheme with SSD detectors by proposing a novel tracking anchor assignment module. With this design SMOT is able to generate tracklets with a constant per-frame runtime. A light-weighted linkage algorithm is then used for online tracklet linking. On three benchmarks of object tracking: Hannah, Music Videos, and MOT17, the proposed SMOT achieves state-of-the-art performance. 我们提出了单次多目标跟踪器（SMOT），这是一种新的跟踪框架，可将任何单次检测器（SSD）模型转换为在线多目标跟踪器，强调同时检测和跟踪目标路径。与现有的检测跟踪方法会受到目标检测器误差的影响，SMOT 采用了最近提出的重新检测跟踪方案。我们通过提出一种新颖的跟踪锚分配模块将该方案与 SSD 检测器结合起来。通过这种设计，SMOT 能够生成具有恒定每帧运行时间的轨迹。然后使用轻量级链接算法进行在线轨迹链接。在三个目标跟踪基准：Hannah、音乐视频和 MOT17 上，所提出的 SMOT 实现了最先进的性能。 本文提出SMOT框架，将单阶段检测器转化为在线多目标跟踪器，实现检测与跟踪的同步。 Wei Li,Yuanjun Xiong,Shuo Yang,Siqi Deng,Wei Xia cs.CV

authors_text: Minz Won,Sergio Oramas,Oriol Nieto,Fabien Gouyon,Xavier Serra
title: Multimodal Metric Learning for Tag-based Music Retrieval
title_zh: 基于标签的音乐检索的多模态度量学习
abstract: Tag-based music retrieval is crucial to browse large-scale music libraries efficiently. Hence, automatic music tagging has been actively explored, mostly as a classification task, which has an inherent limitation: a fixed vocabulary. On the other hand, metric learning enables flexible vocabularies by using pretrained word embeddings as side information. Also, metric learning has already proven its suitability for cross-modal retrieval tasks in other domains (e.g., text-to-image) by jointly learning a multimodal embedding space. In this paper, we investigate three ideas to successfully introduce multimodal metric learning for tag-based music retrieval: elaborate triplet sampling, acoustic and cultural music information, and domain-specific word embeddings. Our experimental results show that the proposed ideas enhance the retrieval system quantitatively, and qualitatively. Furthermore, we release the MSD500, a subset of the Million Song Dataset (MSD) containing 500 cleaned tags, 7 manually annotated tag categories, and user taste profiles.
summary: Tag-based music retrieval is crucial to browse large-scale music libraries efficiently. Hence, automatic music tagging has been actively explored, mostly as a classification task, which has an inherent limitation: a fixed vocabulary. On the other hand, metric learning enables flexible vocabularies by using pretrained word embeddings as side information. Also, metric learning has already proven its suitability for cross-modal retrieval tasks in other domains (e.g., text-to-image) by jointly learning a multimodal embedding space. In this paper, we investigate three ideas to successfully introduce multimodal metric learning for tag-based music retrieval: elaborate triplet sampling, acoustic and cultural music information, and domain-specific word embeddings. Our experimental results show that the proposed ideas enhance the retrieval system quantitatively, and qualitatively. Furthermore, we release the MSD500, a subset of the Million Song Dataset (MSD) containing 500 cleaned tags, 7 manually annotated tag categories, and user taste profiles.
abstract_zh: 基于标签的音乐检索对于高效浏览大规模音乐库至关重要。因此，自动音乐标记已被积极探索，主要作为分类任务，其具有固有的局限性：固定词汇表。另一方面，度量学习通过使用预训练的词嵌入作为辅助信息来实现灵活的词汇。此外，度量学习已经通过联合学习多模态嵌入空间证明了其适用于其他领域（例如文本到图像）的跨模态检索任务。在本文中，我们研究了成功引入基于标签的音乐检索的多模态度量学习的三种想法：精细的三元组采样、声学和文化音乐信息以及特定领域的词嵌入。我们的实验结果表明，所提出的想法在数量和质量上增强了检索系统。此外，我们还发布了 MSD500，它是百万歌曲数据集 (MSD) 的子集，包含 500 个已清理的标签、7 个手动注释的标签类别和用户品味配置文件。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于多模态度量学习的标签音乐检索方法，通过结合音频特征、文化信息和领域特定词嵌入来提升检索效果。
AI.motivation: 传统基于分类的自动音乐标签方法受限于固定词汇表，而度量学习可利用预训练词嵌入实现灵活词汇，且在跨模态检索中表现优异，因此探索多模态度量学习以改进标签音乐检索。
AI.method: 提出三种关键技术：1) 精细的三元组采样策略；2) 融合音频特征与文化音乐信息；3) 使用领域特定词嵌入构建多模态嵌入空间。
AI.result: 实验表明方法在定量和定性指标上均优于基线，同时发布了包含500个清洗标签、7类人工标注标签及用户品味数据的MSD500数据集。
AI.conclusion: 多模态度量学习有效提升了标签音乐检索性能，且新数据集为后续研究提供了重要资源。

authors_text: Thomas Hines,Kazys Stepanas,Fletcher Talbot,Inkyu Sa,Jake Lewis,Emili Hernandez,Navinda Kottege,Nicolas Hudson
title: Virtual Surfaces and Attitude Aware Planning and Behaviours for Negative Obstacle Navigation
title_zh: 负障碍物导航的虚拟表面和姿态感知规划和行为
abstract: This paper presents an autonomous navigation system for ground robots traversing aggressive unstructured terrain through a cohesive arrangement of mapping, deliberative planning and reactive behaviour modules. All systems are aware of terrain slope, visibility and vehicle orientation, enabling robots to recognize, plan and react around unobserved areas and overcome negative obstacles, slopes, steps, overhangs and narrow passageways. This is one of pioneer works to explicitly and simultaneously couple mapping, planning and reactive components in dealing with negative obstacles. The system was deployed on three heterogeneous ground robots for the DARPA Subterranean Challenge, and we present results in Urban and Cave environments, along with simulated scenarios, that demonstrate this approach.
summary: This paper presents an autonomous navigation system for ground robots traversing aggressive unstructured terrain through a cohesive arrangement of mapping, deliberative planning and reactive behaviour modules. All systems are aware of terrain slope, visibility and vehicle orientation, enabling robots to recognize, plan and react around unobserved areas and overcome negative obstacles, slopes, steps, overhangs and narrow passageways. This is one of pioneer works to explicitly and simultaneously couple mapping, planning and reactive components in dealing with negative obstacles. The system was deployed on three heterogeneous ground robots for the DARPA Subterranean Challenge, and we present results in Urban and Cave environments, along with simulated scenarios, that demonstrate this approach.
abstract_zh: 本文提出了一种地面机器人自主导航系统，通过映射、深思熟虑的规划和反应行为模块的紧密结合，穿越侵略性的非结构化地形。所有系统都能感知地形坡度、能见度和车辆方向，使机器人能够识别、规划和应对未观察到的区域，并克服负面障碍物、斜坡、台阶、悬挑和狭窄通道。这是在处理负面障碍时明确且同时耦合绘图、规划和反应组件的先驱工作之一。该系统部署在 DARPA 地下挑战赛的三个异构地面机器人上，我们展示了城市和洞穴环境中的结果以及模拟场景，证明了这种方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种自主导航系统，通过整合映射、规划和反应模块，使地面机器人能够穿越复杂地形中的负障碍物、斜坡和狭窄通道。
AI.motivation: 传统机器人导航方法难以有效处理负障碍物（如凹陷、台阶）和复杂地形，需同时考虑地形坡度、可见度及车辆姿态等多维信息。
AI.method: 系统结合虚拟表面技术与姿态感知规划模块，实时感知地形特征并动态调整路径规划与反应行为，首次实现映射、规划与反应组件在负障碍物场景下的协同工作。
AI.result: 该系统在DARPA地下挑战赛的三个异构机器人上部署，于城市和洞穴环境中验证了其有效性，并通过模拟场景展示了应对负障碍物的鲁棒性。
AI.conclusion: 本文方法通过多模块协同与姿态感知机制，显著提升了机器人在复杂地形中的自主导航能力，为负障碍物场景提供了创新解决方案。

authors_text: Walther Neuper
title: Lucas-Interpretation on Isabelle's Functions
title_zh: 卢卡斯-伊莎贝尔函数的解释
abstract: Software tools of Automated Reasoning are too sophisticated for general use in mathematics education and respective reasoning, while Lucas-Interpretation provides a general concept for integrating such tools into educational software with the purpose to reliably and flexibly check formal input of students. This paper gives the first technically concise description of Lucas-Interpretation at the occasion of migrating a prototype implementation to the function package of the proof assistant Isabelle. The description shows straightforward adaptations of Isabelle's programming language and shows, how simple migration of the interpreter was, since the design (before the function package has been introduced to Isabelle) recognised appropriateness of Isabelle's terms as middle end. The paper gives links into the code in an open repository as invitation to readers for re-using the prototyped code or adopt the general concept. And since the prototype has been designed before the function package was implemented, the paper is an opportunity for recording lessons learned from Isabelle's development of code structure.
summary: Software tools of Automated Reasoning are too sophisticated for general use in mathematics education and respective reasoning, while Lucas-Interpretation provides a general concept for integrating such tools into educational software with the purpose to reliably and flexibly check formal input of students. This paper gives the first technically concise description of Lucas-Interpretation at the occasion of migrating a prototype implementation to the function package of the proof assistant Isabelle. The description shows straightforward adaptations of Isabelle's programming language and shows, how simple migration of the interpreter was, since the design (before the function package has been introduced to Isabelle) recognised appropriateness of Isabelle's terms as middle end. The paper gives links into the code in an open repository as invitation to readers for re-using the prototyped code or adopt the general concept. And since the prototype has been designed before the function package was implemented, the paper is an opportunity for recording lessons learned from Isabelle's development of code structure.
abstract_zh: 自动推理的软件工具过于复杂，无法在数学教育和各自的推理中通用，而卢卡斯解释提供了将此类工具集成到教育软件中的通用概念，目的是可靠、灵活地检查学生的形式输入。本文在将原型实现迁移到证明助手 Isabelle 的功能包时，首次对 Lucas-Interpretation 进行了技术上的简洁描述。该描述显示了对 Isabelle 编程语言的直接改编，并显示了解释器的迁移是多么简单，因为设计（在将功能包引入 Isabelle 之前）认识到 Isabelle 术语作为中间端的适当性。本文提供了开放存储库中代码的链接，邀请读者重用原型代码或采用一般概念。而且由于原型是在功能包实现之前就已经设计好的，所以这篇论文是记录Isabelle代码结构开发过程中的经验教训的一个机会。

AI.tldr: 本文介绍了如何将Lucas-解释器迁移到Isabelle的函数包中，以实现自动化推理工具在数学教育中的应用。
AI.motivation: 自动化推理工具过于复杂，难以在数学教育中广泛使用，因此需要一种通用的方法将这些工具集成到教育软件中，以可靠和灵活地检查学生的正式输入。
AI.method: 本文通过将Lucas-解释器迁移到Isabelle的函数包中，展示了Isabelle编程语言的简单适应方法，并记录了在Isabelle代码结构发展过程中学到的经验。
AI.result: 成功迁移了原型解释器，并展示了Isabelle术语在中间阶段的适用性，同时提供了代码链接供读者复用或改进。
AI.conclusion: 本文为将自动化推理工具集成到教育软件中提供了通用概念，并记录了Isabelle代码结构发展的经验教训。

search_text: Lucas-Interpretation on Isabelle's Functions 卢卡斯-伊莎贝尔函数的解释 Software tools of Automated Reasoning are too sophisticated for general use in mathematics education and respective reasoning, while Lucas-Interpretation provides a general concept for integrating such tools into educational software with the purpose to reliably and flexibly check formal input of students. This paper gives the first technically concise description of Lucas-Interpretation at the occasion of migrating a prototype implementation to the function package of the proof assistant Isabelle. The description shows straightforward adaptations of Isabelle's programming language and shows, how simple migration of the interpreter was, since the design (before the function package has been introduced to Isabelle) recognised appropriateness of Isabelle's terms as middle end. The paper gives links into the code in an open repository as invitation to readers for re-using the prototyped code or adopt the general concept. And since the prototype has been designed before the function package was implemented, the paper is an opportunity for recording lessons learned from Isabelle's development of code structure. 自动推理的软件工具过于复杂，无法在数学教育和各自的推理中通用，而卢卡斯解释提供了将此类工具集成到教育软件中的通用概念，目的是可靠、灵活地检查学生的形式输入。本文在将原型实现迁移到证明助手 Isabelle 的功能包时，首次对 Lucas-Interpretation 进行了技术上的简洁描述。该描述显示了对 Isabelle 编程语言的直接改编，并显示了解释器的迁移是多么简单，因为设计（在将功能包引入 Isabelle 之前）认识到 Isabelle 术语作为中间端的适当性。本文提供了开放存储库中代码的链接，邀请读者重用原型代码或采用一般概念。而且由于原型是在功能包实现之前就已经设计好的，所以这篇论文是记录Isabelle代码结构开发过程中的经验教训的一个机会。 本文介绍了如何将Lucas-解释器迁移到Isabelle的函数包中，以实现自动化推理工具在数学教育中的应用。 Walther Neuper cs.PL,cs.HC

authors_text: Maciej Besta,Michal Podstawski,Linus Groner,Edgar Solomonik,Torsten Hoefler
title: To Push or To Pull: On Reducing Communication and Synchronization in Graph Computations
title_zh: 推还是拉：关于减少图计算中的通信和同步
comment: ef:Proceedings of the 26th ACM International Symposium on High-Performance Parallel and Distributed Computing (HPDC'17), 2017
comments: ef:Proceedings of the 26th ACM International Symposium on High-Performance Parallel and Distributed Computing (HPDC'17), 2017
abstract: We reduce the cost of communication and synchronization in graph processing by analyzing the fastest way to process graphs: pushing the updates to a shared state or pulling the updates to a private state.We investigate the applicability of this push-pull dichotomy to various algorithms and its impact on complexity, performance, and the amount of used locks, atomics, and reads/writes. We consider 11 graph algorithms, 3 programming models, 2 graph abstractions, and various families of graphs. The conducted analysis illustrates surprising differences between push and pull variants of different algorithms in performance, speed of convergence, and code complexity; the insights are backed up by performance data from hardware counters.We use these findings to illustrate which variant is faster for each algorithm and to develop generic strategies that enable even higher speedups. Our insights can be used to accelerate graph processing engines or libraries on both massively-parallel shared-memory machines as well as distributed-memory systems.
summary: We reduce the cost of communication and synchronization in graph processing by analyzing the fastest way to process graphs: pushing the updates to a shared state or pulling the updates to a private state.We investigate the applicability of this push-pull dichotomy to various algorithms and its impact on complexity, performance, and the amount of used locks, atomics, and reads/writes. We consider 11 graph algorithms, 3 programming models, 2 graph abstractions, and various families of graphs. The conducted analysis illustrates surprising differences between push and pull variants of different algorithms in performance, speed of convergence, and code complexity; the insights are backed up by performance data from hardware counters.We use these findings to illustrate which variant is faster for each algorithm and to develop generic strategies that enable even higher speedups. Our insights can be used to accelerate graph processing engines or libraries on both massively-parallel shared-memory machines as well as distributed-memory systems.
abstract_zh: 我们通过分析处理图的最快方法来降低图处理中的通信和同步成本：将更新推送到共享状态或将更新拉到私有状态。我们研究了这种推拉二分法对各种算法的适用性及其对复杂性、性能以及使用的锁、原子和读/写量的影响。我们考虑了 11 种图算法、3 种编程模型、2 种图抽象和各种图族。进行的分析说明了不同算法的推式和拉式变体在性能、收敛速度和代码复杂性方面的惊人差异；这些见解得到了硬件计数器的性能数据的支持。我们使用这些发现来说明每种算法的哪种变体更快，并开发能够实现更高加速的通用策略。我们的见解可用于加速大规模并行共享内存机器以及分布式内存系统上的图形处理引擎或库。

AI.tldr: 本文通过比较推送和拉取两种方式，研究如何减少图计算中的通信和同步开销。
AI.motivation: 图计算在大规模数据处理中普遍存在，但通信和同步开销常成为性能瓶颈，需优化以提高效率。
AI.method: 分析11种图算法、3种编程模型和多种图结构，通过硬件计数器数据对比推送与拉取的性能差异。
AI.result: 发现不同算法在推送/拉取方式下存在显著性能差异，并提出通用策略实现更高加速效果。
AI.conclusion: 根据算法特性选择推送或拉取方式，并结合优化策略可提升图处理系统的性能与可扩展性。

authors_text: Yangyang Guo,Liqiang Nie,Zhiyong Cheng,Qi Tian,Min Zhang
title: Loss re-scaling VQA: Revisiting the LanguagePrior Problem from a Class-imbalance View
title_zh: 损失重新缩放 VQA：从类别不平衡的角度重新审视 LanguagePrior 问题
abstract: Recent studies have pointed out that many well-developed Visual Question Answering (VQA) models are heavily affected by the language prior problem, which refers to making predictions based on the co-occurrence pattern between textual questions and answers instead of reasoning visual contents. To tackle it, most existing methods focus on enhancing visual feature learning to reduce this superficial textual shortcut influence on VQA model decisions. However, limited effort has been devoted to providing an explicit interpretation for its inherent cause. It thus lacks a good guidance for the research community to move forward in a purposeful way, resulting in model construction perplexity in overcoming this non-trivial problem. In this paper, we propose to interpret the language prior problem in VQA from a class-imbalance view. Concretely, we design a novel interpretation scheme whereby the loss of mis-predicted frequent and sparse answers of the same question type is distinctly exhibited during the late training phase. It explicitly reveals why the VQA model tends to produce a frequent yet obviously wrong answer, to a given question whose right answer is sparse in the training set. Based upon this observation, we further develop a novel loss re-scaling approach to assign different weights to each answer based on the training data statistics for computing the final loss. We apply our approach into three baselines and the experimental results on two VQA-CP benchmark datasets evidently demonstrate its effectiveness. In addition, we also justify the validity of the class imbalance interpretation scheme on other computer vision tasks, such as face recognition and image classification.
summary: Recent studies have pointed out that many well-developed Visual Question Answering (VQA) models are heavily affected by the language prior problem, which refers to making predictions based on the co-occurrence pattern between textual questions and answers instead of reasoning visual contents. To tackle it, most existing methods focus on enhancing visual feature learning to reduce this superficial textual shortcut influence on VQA model decisions. However, limited effort has been devoted to providing an explicit interpretation for its inherent cause. It thus lacks a good guidance for the research community to move forward in a purposeful way, resulting in model construction perplexity in overcoming this non-trivial problem. In this paper, we propose to interpret the language prior problem in VQA from a class-imbalance view. Concretely, we design a novel interpretation scheme whereby the loss of mis-predicted frequent and sparse answers of the same question type is distinctly exhibited during the late training phase. It explicitly reveals why the VQA model tends to produce a frequent yet obviously wrong answer, to a given question whose right answer is sparse in the training set. Based upon this observation, we further develop a novel loss re-scaling approach to assign different weights to each answer based on the training data statistics for computing the final loss. We apply our approach into three baselines and the experimental results on two VQA-CP benchmark datasets evidently demonstrate its effectiveness. In addition, we also justify the validity of the class imbalance interpretation scheme on other computer vision tasks, such as face recognition and image classification.
abstract_zh: 最近的研究指出，许多成熟的视觉问答（VQA）模型严重受到语言先验问题的影响，即根据文本问题和答案之间的共现模式进行预测，而不是推理视觉内容。为了解决这个问题，大多数现有方法都专注于增强视觉特征学习，以减少这种肤浅的文本快捷方式对 VQA 模型决策的影响。然而，对其内在原因提供明确解释的努力有限。因此，它缺乏对研究界有目的地前进的良好指导，导致模型构建在克服这个不平凡的问题时陷入困境。在本文中，我们建议从类不平衡的角度解释 VQA 中的语言先验问题。具体来说，我们设计了一种新颖的解释方案，在训练后期阶段，可以明显地表现出同一问题类型的错误预测的频繁和稀疏答案的丢失。它明确揭示了为什么 VQA 模型往往会针对训练集中正确答案稀疏的给定问题产生频繁但明显错误的答案。基于这一观察，我们进一步开发了一种新颖的损失重新调整方法，根据训练数据统计数据为每个答案分配不同的权重，以计算最终损失。我们将我们的方法应用于三个基线，两个 VQA-CP 基准数据集上的实验结果明显证明了其有效性。此外，我们还证明了类不平衡解释方案在其他计算机视觉任务（例如人脸识别和图像分类）上的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文从类别不平衡角度重新审视视觉问答（VQA）中的语言先验问题，提出通过损失重缩放方法缓解模型对高频答案的过度依赖。
AI.motivation: 现有方法主要通过增强视觉特征学习来应对语言先验问题，但缺乏对问题本质原因的明确解释，导致模型设计缺乏针对性指导。
AI.method: 设计新型损失重缩放方案，在训练后期显式放大稀有正确答案的损失权重，根据训练数据统计动态调整答案损失计算权重。
AI.result: 在VQA-CP基准数据集上显著提升模型性能，并验证了类别不平衡解释框架在人脸识别和图像分类任务中的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 语言先验问题本质是类别不平衡导致的模型偏差，损失重缩放方法为解决该问题提供了可解释且有效的技术路径。

To tackle it, most existing methods focus on enhancing visual feature learning to reduce this superficial textual shortcut influence on VQA model decisions. However, limited effort has been devoted to providing an explicit interpretation for its inherent cause. It thus lacks a good guidance for the research community to move forward in a purposeful way, resulting in model construction perplexity in overcoming this non-trivial problem. In this paper, we propose to interpret the language prior problem in VQA from a class-imbalance view. Concretely, we design a novel interpretation scheme whereby the loss of mis-predicted frequent and sparse answers of the same question type is distinctly exhibited during the late training phase. It explicitly reveals why the VQA model tends to produce a frequent yet obviously wrong answer, to a given question whose right answer is sparse in the training set. Based upon this observation, we further develop a novel loss re-scaling approach to assign different weights to each answer based on the training data statistics for computing the final loss. We apply our approach into three baselines and the experimental results on two VQA-CP benchmark datasets evidently demonstrate its effectiveness. In addition, we also justify the validity of the class imbalance interpretation scheme on other computer vision tasks, such as face recognition and image classification. 最近的研究指出，许多成熟的视觉问答（VQA）模型严重受到语言先验问题的影响，即根据文本问题和答案之间的共现模式进行预测，而不是推理视觉内容。为了解决这个问题，大多数现有方法都专注于增强视觉特征学习，以减少这种肤浅的文本快捷方式对 VQA 模型决策的影响。然而，对其内在原因提供明确解释的努力有限。因此，它缺乏对研究界有目的地前进的良好指导，导致模型构建在克服这个不平凡的问题时陷入困境。在本文中，我们建议从类不平衡的角度解释 VQA 中的语言先验问题。具体来说，我们设计了一种新颖的解释方案，在训练后期阶段，可以明显地表现出同一问题类型的错误预测的频繁和稀疏答案的丢失。它明确揭示了为什么 VQA 模型往往会针对训练集中正确答案稀疏的给定问题产生频繁但明显错误的答案。基于这一观察，我们进一步开发了一种新颖的损失重新调整方法，根据训练数据统计数据为每个答案分配不同的权重，以计算最终损失。我们将我们的方法应用于三个基线，两个 VQA-CP 基准数据集上的实验结果明显证明了其有效性。此外，我们还证明了类不平衡解释方案在其他计算机视觉任务（例如人脸识别和图像分类）上的有效性。 本文从类别不平衡角度重新审视视觉问答（VQA）中的语言先验问题，提出通过损失重缩放方法缓解模型对高频答案的过度依赖。 Yangyang Guo,Liqiang Nie,Zhiyong Cheng,Qi Tian,Min Zhang cs.CV

authors_text: Seo Woo Han,Doug Young Suh
title: PIINET: A 360-degree Panoramic Image Inpainting Network Using a Cube Map
title_zh: PIINET：使用立方体地图的 360 度全景图像修复网络
abstract: Inpainting has been continuously studied in the field of computer vision. As artificial intelligence technology developed, deep learning technology was introduced in inpainting research, helping to improve performance. Currently, the input target of an inpainting algorithm using deep learning has been studied from a single image to a video. However, deep learning-based inpainting technology for panoramic images has not been actively studied. We propose a 360-degree panoramic image inpainting method using generative adversarial networks (GANs). The proposed network inputs a 360-degree equirectangular format panoramic image converts it into a cube map format, which has relatively little distortion and uses it as a training network. Since the cube map format is used, the correlation of the six sides of the cube map should be considered. Therefore, all faces of the cube map are used as input for the whole discriminative network, and each face of the cube map is used as input for the slice discriminative network to determine the authenticity of the generated image. The proposed network performed qualitatively better than existing single-image inpainting algorithms and baseline algorithms.
summary: Inpainting has been continuously studied in the field of computer vision. As artificial intelligence technology developed, deep learning technology was introduced in inpainting research, helping to improve performance. Currently, the input target of an inpainting algorithm using deep learning has been studied from a single image to a video. However, deep learning-based inpainting technology for panoramic images has not been actively studied. We propose a 360-degree panoramic image inpainting method using generative adversarial networks (GANs). The proposed network inputs a 360-degree equirectangular format panoramic image converts it into a cube map format, which has relatively little distortion and uses it as a training network. Since the cube map format is used, the correlation of the six sides of the cube map should be considered. Therefore, all faces of the cube map are used as input for the whole discriminative network, and each face of the cube map is used as input for the slice discriminative network to determine the authenticity of the generated image. The proposed network performed qualitatively better than existing single-image inpainting algorithms and baseline algorithms.
abstract_zh: 修复在计算机视觉领域一直在不断地被研究。随着人工智能技术的发展，深度学习技术被引入修复研究中，有助于提高性能。目前，使用深度学习的修复算法的输入目标已经从单个图像到视频进行了研究。然而，基于深度学习的全景图像修复技术尚未得到积极研究。我们提出了一种使用生成对抗网络（GAN）的 360 度全景图像修复方法。所提出的网络输入360度等距矩形格式全景图像，将其转换为畸变相对较小的立方体贴图格式，并将其用作训练网络。由于使用立方体贴图格式，因此应考虑立方体贴图六个边的相关性。因此，立方体图的所有面都用作整个判别网络的输入，立方体图的每个面都用作切片判别网络的输入，以确定生成图像的真实性。所提出的网络在质量上优于现有的单图像修复算法和基线算法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于立方体贴图的360度全景图像修复网络PIINET，利用生成对抗网络（GAN）改进全景图像修复效果。
AI.motivation: 现有深度学习修复技术主要针对单张图像和视频，但对全景图像的修复研究不足，而全景图像因投影畸变导致修复难度更大。
AI.method: 将360度等距投影全景图转换为立方体贴图格式以减少畸变，设计全立方体贴图判别网络与分面判别网络，通过多尺度对抗损失联合优化生成质量。
AI.result: 在定性实验中，PIINET生成的修复结果在视觉连续性和细节还原度上优于现有单图修复算法和基线方法。
AI.conclusion: 立方体贴图表示结合多尺度对抗训练能有效提升全景图像修复质量，为全景内容生成提供了新思路。

authors_text: Gideon Kowadlo,Abdelrahman Ahmed,David Rawlinson
title: Unsupervised One-shot Learning of Both Specific Instances and Generalised Classes with a Hippocampal Architecture
title_zh: 使用海马架构对特定实例和泛化类进行无监督一次性学习
comment: To appear at AI 2020 (Australasian Joint Conference on Artificial Intelligence - http://www.ajcai2020.net/)
comments: To appear at AI 2020 (Australasian Joint Conference on Artificial Intelligence - http://www.ajcai2020.net/)
abstract: Established experimental procedures for one-shot machine learning do not test the ability to learn or remember specific instances of classes, a key feature of animal intelligence. Distinguishing specific instances is necessary for many real-world tasks, such as remembering which cup belongs to you. Generalisation within classes conflicts with the ability to separate instances of classes, making it difficult to achieve both capabilities within a single architecture. We propose an extension to the standard Omniglot classification-generalisation framework that additionally tests the ability to distinguish specific instances after one exposure and introduces noise and occlusion corruption. Learning is defined as an ability to classify as well as recall training samples. Complementary Learning Systems (CLS) is a popular model of mammalian brain regions believed to play a crucial role in learning from a single exposure to a stimulus. We created an artificial neural network implementation of CLS and applied it to the extended Omniglot benchmark. Our unsupervised model demonstrates comparable performance to existing supervised ANNs on the Omniglot classification task (requiring generalisation), without the need for domain-specific inductive biases. On the extended Omniglot instance-recognition task, the same model also demonstrates significantly better performance than a baseline nearest-neighbour approach, given partial occlusion and noise.
summary: Established experimental procedures for one-shot machine learning do not test the ability to learn or remember specific instances of classes, a key feature of animal intelligence. Distinguishing specific instances is necessary for many real-world tasks, such as remembering which cup belongs to you. Generalisation within classes conflicts with the ability to separate instances of classes, making it difficult to achieve both capabilities within a single architecture. We propose an extension to the standard Omniglot classification-generalisation framework that additionally tests the ability to distinguish specific instances after one exposure and introduces noise and occlusion corruption. Learning is defined as an ability to classify as well as recall training samples. Complementary Learning Systems (CLS) is a popular model of mammalian brain regions believed to play a crucial role in learning from a single exposure to a stimulus. We created an artificial neural network implementation of CLS and applied it to the extended Omniglot benchmark. Our unsupervised model demonstrates comparable performance to existing supervised ANNs on the Omniglot classification task (requiring generalisation), without the need for domain-specific inductive biases. On the extended Omniglot instance-recognition task, the same model also demonstrates significantly better performance than a baseline nearest-neighbour approach, given partial occlusion and noise.
abstract_zh: 已建立的一次性机器学习实验程序并不测试学习或记住特定类别实例的能力，而这是动物智力的一个关键特征。对于许多现实世界的任务来说，区分特定实例是必要的，例如记住哪个杯子属于你。类内的泛化与分离类实例的能力相冲突，使得很难在单个体系结构中实现这两种功能。我们提出了对标准 Omniglot 分类泛化框架的扩展，该框架还测试了一次曝光后区分特定实例的能力，并引入了噪声和遮挡损坏。学习被定义为分类和回忆训练样本的能力。补充学习系统（CLS）是一种流行的哺乳动物大脑区域模型，人们认为它在单次刺激学习中发挥着至关重要的作用。我们创建了 CLS 的人工神经网络实现，并将其应用于扩展的 Omniglot 基准测试。我们的无监督模型在 Omniglot 分类任务（需要泛化）上表现出与现有监督 ANN 相当的性能，而不需要特定领域的归纳偏差。在扩展的 Omniglot 实例识别任务中，考虑到部分遮挡和噪声，同一模型还表现出比基线最近邻方法明显更好的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种无监督的一次性学习模型，能够同时学习特定实例和泛化类别，基于海马体架构实现。
AI.motivation: 现有单次学习方法无法区分同类中的具体实例，而这是动物智能的关键能力，且泛化能力与实例区分存在冲突。
AI.method: 扩展Omniglot框架以测试实例区分能力，引入噪声和遮挡，基于互补学习系统（CLS）构建无监督神经网络模型。
AI.result: 模型在Omniglot分类任务中表现与监督模型相当，在实例识别任务中于噪声遮挡下显著优于最近邻方法。
AI.conclusion: 该模型有效结合了泛化与实例记忆能力，无需监督信号和领域偏置，适用于现实场景中的单次学习任务。

authors_text: Jason Taylor
title: Latent Space Oddity: Exploring Latent Spaces to Design Guitar Timbres
title_zh: 潜在空间怪异：探索潜在空间来设计吉他音色
comment: 3 pages, 1 figure. To appear in the 2020 NeurIps Workshop on Machine Learning for Creativity and Design
comments: 3 pages, 1 figure. To appear in the 2020 NeurIps Workshop on Machine Learning for Creativity and Design
abstract: We introduce a novel convolutional network architecture with an interpretable latent space for modeling guitar amplifiers. Leveraging domain knowledge of popular amplifiers spanning a range of styles, the proposed system intuitively combines or subtracts characteristics of different amplifiers, allowing musicians to design entirely new guitar timbres.
summary: We introduce a novel convolutional network architecture with an interpretable latent space for modeling guitar amplifiers. Leveraging domain knowledge of popular amplifiers spanning a range of styles, the proposed system intuitively combines or subtracts characteristics of different amplifiers, allowing musicians to design entirely new guitar timbres.
abstract_zh: 我们引入了一种新颖的卷积网络架构，具有可解释的潜在空间，用于对吉他放大器进行建模。利用涵盖各种风格的流行放大器的领域知识，所提出的系统直观地组合或减去不同放大器的特性，使音乐家能够设计全新的吉他音色。

AI.motivation: 传统吉他音色设计缺乏直观的工具，本文旨在通过结合领域知识，为音乐家提供一种可解释且灵活的音色设计方法。
AI.method: 基于流行放大器的领域知识，构建可解释的潜在空间，通过加减不同放大器特征实现音色的组合与设计。
AI.result: 实现了能够直观设计全新吉他音色的系统，验证了潜在空间在音色建模中的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过可解释的潜在空间和领域知识结合，为吉他音色设计提供了创新的解决方案。

authors_text: K. Aditya Mohan,Alan D. Kaplan
title: AutoAtlas: Neural Network for 3D Unsupervised Partitioning and Representation Learning
title_zh: AutoAtlas：用于 3D 无监督分区和表示学习的神经网络
comment: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
comments: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: We present a novel neural network architecture called AutoAtlas for fully unsupervised partitioning and representation learning of 3D brain Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) volumes. AutoAtlas consists of two neural network components: one neural network to perform multi-label partitioning based on local texture in the volume, and a second neural network to compress the information contained within each partition. We train both of these components simultaneously by optimizing a loss function that is designed to promote accurate reconstruction of each partition, while encouraging spatially smooth and contiguous partitioning, and discouraging relatively small partitions. We show that the partitions adapt to the subject specific structural variations of brain tissue while consistently appearing at similar spatial locations across subjects. AutoAtlas also produces very low dimensional features that represent local texture of each partition. We demonstrate prediction of metadata associated with each subject using the derived feature representations and compare the results to prediction using features derived from FreeSurfer anatomical parcellation. Since our features are intrinsically linked to distinct partitions, we can then map values of interest, such as partition-specific feature importance scores onto the brain for visualization.
summary: We present a novel neural network architecture called AutoAtlas for fully unsupervised partitioning and representation learning of 3D brain Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) volumes. AutoAtlas consists of two neural network components: one neural network to perform multi-label partitioning based on local texture in the volume, and a second neural network to compress the information contained within each partition. We train both of these components simultaneously by optimizing a loss function that is designed to promote accurate reconstruction of each partition, while encouraging spatially smooth and contiguous partitioning, and discouraging relatively small partitions. We show that the partitions adapt to the subject specific structural variations of brain tissue while consistently appearing at similar spatial locations across subjects. AutoAtlas also produces very low dimensional features that represent local texture of each partition. We demonstrate prediction of metadata associated with each subject using the derived feature representations and compare the results to prediction using features derived from FreeSurfer anatomical parcellation. Since our features are intrinsically linked to distinct partitions, we can then map values of interest, such as partition-specific feature importance scores onto the brain for visualization.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了一种称为 AutoAtlas 的新型神经网络架构，用于 3D 脑磁共振成像 (MRI) 体积的完全无监督分区和表示学习。 AutoAtlas 由两个神经网络组件组成：一个神经网络用于根据体积中的局部纹理执行多标签分区，第二个神经网络用于压缩每个分区中包含的信息。我们通过优化损失函数来同时训练这两个组件，该损失函数旨在促进每个分区的准确重建，同时鼓励空间平滑和连续的分区，并阻止相对较小的分区。我们表明，分区适应受试者特定的脑组织结构变化，同时一致地出现在受试者之间相似的空间位置。 AutoAtlas 还生成非常低维的特征，代表每个分区的局部纹理。我们使用派生的特征表示来演示与每个主题相关的元数据的预测，并将结果与​​使用从 FreeSurfer 解剖分区派生的特征进行的预测进行比较。由于我们的特征本质上与不同的分区相关，因此我们可以将感兴趣的值（例如特定于分区的特征重要性分数）映射到大脑上以进行可视化。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为AutoAtlas的神经网络架构，用于对3D脑部MRI体积进行无监督分割和表征学习。
AI.motivation: 现有方法在处理脑部结构差异和生成一致空间分区方面存在局限性，需开发一种能适应个体差异且保持分区空间一致性的方法。
AI.method: AutoAtlas包含两个神经网络组件：一个基于局部纹理进行多标签分割，另一个压缩分区信息。通过优化损失函数同时训练两者，以促进准确重建、空间平滑连续分区并避免小分区。
AI.result: 分区能适应个体脑组织结构差异且在不同受试者中保持相似空间位置，生成低维纹理特征并成功预测元数据，优于FreeSurfer的解剖分割特征。
AI.conclusion: AutoAtlas有效实现了脑MRI的无监督分割与表征学习，生成的特征支持可视化和元数据预测，为神经影像分析提供了新工具。

authors_text: Roger Granda,Tinne Tuytelaars,Jose Oramas
title: Can the state of relevant neurons in a deep neural networks serve as indicators for detecting adversarial attacks?
title_zh: 深度神经网络中相关神经元的状态能否作为检测对抗性攻击的指标？
abstract: We present a method for adversarial attack detection based on the inspection of a sparse set of neurons. We follow the hypothesis that adversarial attacks introduce imperceptible perturbations in the input and that these perturbations change the state of neurons relevant for the concepts modelled by the attacked model. Therefore, monitoring the status of these neurons would enable the detection of adversarial attacks. Focusing on the image classification task, our method identifies neurons that are relevant for the classes predicted by the model. A deeper qualitative inspection of these sparse set of neurons indicates that their state changes in the presence of adversarial samples. Moreover, quantitative results from our empirical evaluation indicate that our method is capable of recognizing adversarial samples, produced by state-of-the-art attack methods, with comparable accuracy to that of state-of-the-art detectors.
summary: We present a method for adversarial attack detection based on the inspection of a sparse set of neurons. We follow the hypothesis that adversarial attacks introduce imperceptible perturbations in the input and that these perturbations change the state of neurons relevant for the concepts modelled by the attacked model. Therefore, monitoring the status of these neurons would enable the detection of adversarial attacks. Focusing on the image classification task, our method identifies neurons that are relevant for the classes predicted by the model. A deeper qualitative inspection of these sparse set of neurons indicates that their state changes in the presence of adversarial samples. Moreover, quantitative results from our empirical evaluation indicate that our method is capable of recognizing adversarial samples, produced by state-of-the-art attack methods, with comparable accuracy to that of state-of-the-art detectors.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了一种基于稀疏神经元集检查的对抗性攻击检测方法。我们遵循这样的假设：对抗性攻击在输入中引入了难以察觉的扰动，并且这些扰动改变了与受攻击模型建模的概念相关的神经元的状态。因此，监测这些神经元的状态将能够检测对抗性攻击。专注于图像分类任务，我们的方法识别与模型预测的类别相关的神经元。对这些稀疏神经元组进行更深入的定性检查表明，它们的状态在存在对抗性样本的情况下发生了变化。此外，我们的实证评估的定量结果表明，我们的方法能够识别由最先进的攻击方法产生的对抗性样本，其准确度与最先进的检测器相当。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于深度神经网络中相关神经元状态的对抗攻击检测方法。
AI.motivation: 对抗攻击通过微小扰动影响模型决策，这些扰动会改变模型中与概念相关的神经元状态，监测这些状态可实现攻击检测。
AI.method: 聚焦图像分类任务，识别模型预测类别相关的神经元，通过分析这些稀疏神经元的状态变化检测对抗样本。
AI.result: 实验表明该方法能以与现有最先进检测器相当的准确率识别多种先进攻击方法生成的对抗样本。
AI.conclusion: 神经元状态可作为有效指标用于对抗攻击检测，为轻量级检测提供了新思路。

authors_text: Zhenwei Dai,Aditya Desai,Reinhard Heckel,Anshumali Shrivastava
title: Active Sampling Count Sketch (ASCS) for Online Sparse Estimation of a Trillion Scale Covariance Matrix
title_zh: 用于万亿级协方差矩阵在线稀疏估计的主动采样计数草图 (ASCS)
abstract: Estimating and storing the covariance (or correlation) matrix of high-dimensional data is computationally challenging because both memory and computational requirements scale quadratically with the dimension. Fortunately, high-dimensional covariance matrices as observed in text, click-through, meta-genomics datasets, etc are often sparse. In this paper, we consider the problem of efficient sparse estimation of covariance matrices with possibly trillions of entries. The size of the datasets we target requires the algorithm to be online, as more than one pass over the data is prohibitive. In this paper, we propose Active Sampling Count Sketch (ASCS), an online and one-pass sketching algorithm, that recovers the large entries of the covariance matrix accurately. Count Sketch (CS), and other sub-linear compressed sensing algorithms, offer a natural solution to the problem in theory. However, vanilla CS does not work well in practice due to a low signal-to-noise ratio (SNR). At the heart of our approach is a novel active sampling strategy that increases the SNR of classical CS. We demonstrate the practicality of our algorithm with synthetic data and real-world high dimensional datasets. ASCS significantly improves over vanilla CS, demonstrating the merit of our active sampling strategy.
summary: Estimating and storing the covariance (or correlation) matrix of high-dimensional data is computationally challenging because both memory and computational requirements scale quadratically with the dimension. Fortunately, high-dimensional covariance matrices as observed in text, click-through, meta-genomics datasets, etc are often sparse. In this paper, we consider the problem of efficient sparse estimation of covariance matrices with possibly trillions of entries. The size of the datasets we target requires the algorithm to be online, as more than one pass over the data is prohibitive. In this paper, we propose Active Sampling Count Sketch (ASCS), an online and one-pass sketching algorithm, that recovers the large entries of the covariance matrix accurately. Count Sketch (CS), and other sub-linear compressed sensing algorithms, offer a natural solution to the problem in theory. However, vanilla CS does not work well in practice due to a low signal-to-noise ratio (SNR). At the heart of our approach is a novel active sampling strategy that increases the SNR of classical CS. We demonstrate the practicality of our algorithm with synthetic data and real-world high dimensional datasets. ASCS significantly improves over vanilla CS, demonstrating the merit of our active sampling strategy.
abstract_zh: 估计和存储高维数据的协方差（或相关）矩阵在计算上具有挑战性，因为内存和计算要求都与维度成二次方缩放。幸运的是，在文本、点击、元基因组数据集等中观察到的高维协方差矩阵通常是稀疏的。在本文中，我们考虑了可能有数万亿个条目的协方差矩阵的有效稀疏估计问题。我们目标数据集的大小要求算法在线，因为多次传递数据是禁止的。在本文中，我们提出了主动采样计数草图（ASCS），这是一种在线一次性草图算法，可以准确地恢复协方差矩阵的大条目。 Count Sketch (CS) 和其他次线性压缩感知算法在理论上为该问题提供了自然的解决方案。然而，由于信噪比 (SNR) 较低，普通 CS 在实践中效果不佳。我们方法的核心是一种新颖的主动采样策略，可以提高经典 CS 的 SNR。我们通过合成数据和现实世界的高维数据集证明了我们的算法的实用性。 ASCS 比普通 CS 有了显着改进，证明了我们主动采样策略的优点。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为Active Sampling Count Sketch (ASCS)的在线算法，用于高效估计万亿规模稀疏协方差矩阵。
AI.motivation: 高维数据的协方差矩阵估计面临存储和计算资源的挑战，传统方法如Count Sketch因信噪比低导致实际效果不佳，需改进稀疏估计方法。
AI.method: 通过引入主动采样策略优化经典Count Sketch算法，提升信噪比，实现对协方差矩阵大值项的准确恢复。
AI.result: 在合成数据和真实高维数据集上验证了ASCS的有效性，显著优于传统Count Sketch方法。
AI.conclusion: ASCS通过主动采样策略有效解决了大规模稀疏协方差矩阵的在线估计问题，具有实际应用价值。

authors_text: Tai Hoang,Ngo Anh Vien
title: Bayes-Adaptive Deep Model-Based Policy Optimisation
title_zh: 贝叶斯自适应深度模型策略优化
abstract: We introduce a Bayesian (deep) model-based reinforcement learning method (RoMBRL) that can capture model uncertainty to achieve sample-efficient policy optimisation. We propose to formulate the model-based policy optimisation problem as a Bayes-adaptive Markov decision process (BAMDP). RoMBRL maintains model uncertainty via belief distributions through a deep Bayesian neural network whose samples are generated via stochastic gradient Hamiltonian Monte Carlo. Uncertainty is propagated through simulations controlled by sampled models and history-based policies. As beliefs are encoded in visited histories, we propose a history-based policy network that can be end-to-end trained to generalise across history space and will be trained using recurrent Trust-Region Policy Optimisation. We show that RoMBRL outperforms existing approaches on many challenging control benchmark tasks in terms of sample complexity and task performance. The source code of this paper is also publicly available on https://github.com/thobotics/RoMBRL.
summary: We introduce a Bayesian (deep) model-based reinforcement learning method (RoMBRL) that can capture model uncertainty to achieve sample-efficient policy optimisation. We propose to formulate the model-based policy optimisation problem as a Bayes-adaptive Markov decision process (BAMDP). RoMBRL maintains model uncertainty via belief distributions through a deep Bayesian neural network whose samples are generated via stochastic gradient Hamiltonian Monte Carlo. Uncertainty is propagated through simulations controlled by sampled models and history-based policies. As beliefs are encoded in visited histories, we propose a history-based policy network that can be end-to-end trained to generalise across history space and will be trained using recurrent Trust-Region Policy Optimisation. We show that RoMBRL outperforms existing approaches on many challenging control benchmark tasks in terms of sample complexity and task performance. The source code of this paper is also publicly available on https://github.com/thobotics/RoMBRL.
abstract_zh: 我们引入了一种基于贝叶斯（深度）模型的强化学习方法（RoMBRL），该方法可以捕获模型不确定性以实现样本有效的策略优化。我们建议将基于模型的策略优化问题表述为贝叶斯自适应马尔可夫决策过程（BAMDP）。 RoMBRL 通过深度贝叶斯神经网络的置信分布来维持模型的不确定性，该神经网络的样本是通过随机梯度哈密顿蒙特卡罗生成的。不确定性通过采样模型和基于历史的策略控制的模拟来传播。由于信念被编码在访问的历史中，我们提出了一个基于历史的策略网络，可以进行端到端训练以在历史空间中进行泛化，并将使用循环信任区域策略优化进行训练。我们证明，在样本复杂性和任务性能方面，RoMBRL 在许多具有挑战性的控制基准任务上优于现有方法。本文的源代码也可在 https://github.com/thobotics/RoMBRL 上公开获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种贝叶斯自适应深度模型基于策略优化方法（RoMBRL），通过捕捉模型不确定性实现样本高效的策略优化。
AI.motivation: 现有模型基于策略优化方法在样本效率和模型不确定性处理上存在不足，需结合贝叶斯方法提升鲁棒性与效率。
AI.method: 将问题建模为贝叶斯自适应马尔可夫决策过程（BAMDP），采用深度贝叶斯神经网络与随机梯度哈密顿蒙特卡洛生成模型样本，通过历史编码策略网络与递归TRPO进行端到端训练。
AI.result: RoMBRL在多个控制基准任务中优于现有方法，在样本复杂度和任务性能上表现更优。
AI.conclusion: RoMBRL有效结合贝叶斯推理与深度强化学习，提升模型不确定性处理能力与样本效率，代码开源促进实际应用。

We propose to formulate the model-based policy optimisation problem as a Bayes-adaptive Markov decision process (BAMDP). authors_text: Shubhang Bhatnagar,Sachin Goyal,Darshan Tank,Amit Sethi
title: PAL : Pretext-based Active Learning
title_zh: PAL：基于借口的主动学习
abstract: The goal of pool-based active learning is to judiciously select a fixed-sized subset of unlabeled samples from a pool to query an oracle for their labels, in order to maximize the accuracy of a supervised learner. However, the unsaid requirement that the oracle should always assign correct labels is unreasonable for most situations. We propose an active learning technique for deep neural networks that is more robust to mislabeling than the previously proposed techniques. Previous techniques rely on the task network itself to estimate the novelty of the unlabeled samples, but learning the task (generalization) and selecting samples (out-of-distribution detection) can be conflicting goals. We use a separate network to score the unlabeled samples for selection. The scoring network relies on self-supervision for modeling the distribution of the labeled samples to reduce the dependency on potentially noisy labels. To counter the paucity of data, we also deploy another head on the scoring network for regularization via multi-task learning and use an unusual self-balancing hybrid scoring function. Furthermore, we divide each query into sub-queries before labeling to ensure that the query has diverse samples. In addition to having a higher tolerance to mislabeling of samples by the oracle, the resultant technique also produces competitive accuracy in the absence of label noise. The technique also handles the introduction of new classes on-the-fly well by temporarily increasing the sampling rate of these classes.
summary: The goal of pool-based active learning is to judiciously select a fixed-sized subset of unlabeled samples from a pool to query an oracle for their labels, in order to maximize the accuracy of a supervised learner. However, the unsaid requirement that the oracle should always assign correct labels is unreasonable for most situations. We propose an active learning technique for deep neural networks that is more robust to mislabeling than the previously proposed techniques. Previous techniques rely on the task network itself to estimate the novelty of the unlabeled samples, but learning the task (generalization) and selecting samples (out-of-distribution detection) can be conflicting goals. We use a separate network to score the unlabeled samples for selection. The scoring network relies on self-supervision for modeling the distribution of the labeled samples to reduce the dependency on potentially noisy labels. To counter the paucity of data, we also deploy another head on the scoring network for regularization via multi-task learning and use an unusual self-balancing hybrid scoring function. Furthermore, we divide each query into sub-queries before labeling to ensure that the query has diverse samples. In addition to having a higher tolerance to mislabeling of samples by the oracle, the resultant technique also produces competitive accuracy in the absence of label noise. The technique also handles the introduction of new classes on-the-fly well by temporarily increasing the sampling rate of these classes.
abstract_zh: 基于池的主动学习的目标是明智地从池中选择固定大小的未标记样本子集来查询预言机的标签，以最大限度地提高监督学习器的准确性。然而，在大多数情况下，预言机应始终分配正确标签的未提及的要求是不合理的。我们提出了一种用于深度神经网络的主动学习技术，该技术比之前提出的技术更能抵抗错误标记。以前的技术依赖于任务网络本身来估计未标记样本的新颖性，但学习任务（泛化）和选择样本（分布外检测）可能是相互冲突的目标。我们使用单独的网络对未标记的样本进行评分以供选择。评分网络依靠自我监督来对标记样本的分布进行建模，以减少对潜在噪声标签的依赖。为了解决数据缺乏的问题，我们还在评分网络上部署了另一个头，通过多任务学习进行正则化，并使用不寻常的自平衡混合评分函数。此外，我们在标记之前将每个查询划分为子查询，以确保查询具有多样化的样本。除了对预言机错误标记样本具有更高的容忍度之外，所得到的技术还在没有标签噪声的情况下产生了有竞争力的准确性。该技术还可以通过暂时增加这些类的采样率来很好地处理动态引入新类。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于预文本的主动学习方法（PAL），旨在提高深度神经网络在存在标签噪声情况下的鲁棒性。
AI.motivation: 传统主动学习方法依赖任务网络本身评估样本新颖性，但学习任务与检测分布外样本的目标存在冲突，且对标注噪声敏感。
AI.method: 设计独立的评分网络通过自监督建模已标注样本分布，结合多任务学习正则化和混合评分函数，并通过子查询划分确保样本多样性。
AI.result: 该方法在存在标签噪声时表现更鲁棒，无噪声环境下准确率具有竞争力，且能通过临时提升新类采样率实现动态类别处理。
AI.conclusion: PAL通过解耦任务学习与样本选择过程，有效缓解了标注噪声影响，在主动学习领域具有显著优势。
